keyPoints: Frame the chapter around the 3-month cross-domain transfer test of post-task active-recall prompts after AI-assisted work
Anchor design in retrieval-practice literature with AI-context specifics (prompt timing, feedback, spacing, and domain transfer demands)
Distinguish a three-arm field design: Treatment with active-recall prompts, active-control, and no prompts, with cross-domain transfer tasks as the primary endpoint
Prioritize cross-domain transfer measures at 3 months as the primary outcome, with effect sizes and preregistration
Log and report prompt quality, engagement, feedback content, and potential moderating factors like prior knowledge
Articulate actionable design heuristics for designers and concrete use-protocols for knowledge workers, grounded in retrieval-practice theory and transfer science
Acknowledge gaps and caveats, especially field evidence scarcity for AI-enabled 3-month cross-domain transfer, and outline rigorous steps to address them
